rows.title: Brandenburg DPA Joins EDPB Coordinated Enforcement Action on Right to Erasure, Examining Ten Housing Companies
rows.summary:
The Landesbeauftragte für den Datenschutz Brandenburg participated in a European Data Protection Board coordinated enforcement action examining compliance with the right to erasure under Article 17 GDPR, selecting ten larger housing companies based in Brandenburg for review.

On 18 February 2026, the European Data Protection Board published the results of its 2025 Coordinated Enforcement Framework action on the right to erasure (Art. 17 GDPR). A total of 32 supervisory authorities across Europe participated, sending a standardised questionnaire to controllers. The EDPB notes that 764 bodies, companies and other controllers responded in total, including 60 in Germany. German participating authorities included those from Baden-Württemberg, Brandenburg, Mecklenburg-Vorpommern, Lower Saxony, North Rhine-Westphalia, Rhineland-Palatinate, and the Federal Commissioner for Data Protection and Freedom of Information.

The EDPB finds that controllers faced several recurring challenges in implementing the right to erasure. These included the absence of clear internal rules on when and how to delete personal data, a lack of internal staff training, and legal uncertainty regarding the application of exemptions and retention periods. The EDPB further identifies uncertainty among controllers concerning the deletion of personal data held in back-up systems and the requirements for anonymisation. The findings have been incorporated into a final report, which includes recommendations derived from the review. A German annex to the report additionally sets out a range of best practices.

Regarding Brandenburg's participation, Commissioner Dagmar Hartge states that the housing companies examined demonstrated a high level of cooperation, and that the results underline the importance of establishing internal deletion routines. Hartge states that tenants and prospective tenants are required to be able to rely on their personal data not being processed without authorisation. The Landesbeauftragte for Brandenburg notes that it will follow up by sending individual advisory letters to the housing companies that participated in the questionnaire, with the aim of raising awareness of the conditions governing the right to erasure. The 2025 coordinated action on the right to erasure is the fourth initiative under the CEF, following earlier actions in 2023 on data protection officers and in 2024 on the right of access.
